3.why: The rotation works on an n by n grid.

4.invariant: Cells on the diagonal stay put.

5.happening: Take column {col}, to the right of the diagonal.
5.why: Only the upper triangle is swapped, so each pair swaps once.
5.invariant: Lower triangle is reached by its mirror.

7.happening: Take a row of the transposed matrix.
7.why: Reversing each row finishes the clockwise turn.
7.invariant: Transpose plus row-reverse equals a 90-degree rotation.

8.why: It flips the transposed row into rotated order.
8.invariant: Each reversed row is in final position.

loop.why: Visit each swap pair, then each row.

update.happening: Swap a cell with its diagonal mirror.
